raw_response: I agree with the consensus that option B, the facial nerve, supplies the skin of the entire upper lip. My agreement stems from the fact that although the maxillary branch of the trigeminal nerve contributes significantly to the sensory innervation of much of the face including parts of the upper lip, it cannot be said to supply every single point on the upper lip without exception. The facial nerve fills in any potential gaps via its own branches, thus ensuring complete sensory coverage across the entire upper lip surface. Given this thorough explanation covering both major contributors—trigeminal and facial nerves—the correct answer indeed stands firm as B. Answer choice: B.
